Seed LibraryThe AlmanacMembershipShopVisit
Greenhouse
Strafford, Vermont

Come in season.

The farm sits on forty acres of old pasture in the hills outside Strafford. We are open to visitors during the growing season — on scheduled open days and by appointment for members.

The Farm

18 Old Mill Road, Strafford

We are 45 minutes from both Burlington and Hanover. The farm is at the end of a long dirt road — worth it, we are told. There is ample parking in the meadow by the barn, and the seed garden is immediately visible from the drive.

The barn, where the seed-drying happens and where we print the Almanac on our Risograph, is open on all scheduled open days. Oliver is usually there and happy to show you how it works.

Address

18 Old Mill Road, Strafford, VT 05072

From Burlington

I-89 South to Exit 2, then VT-14 South. 42 miles, approximately 50 minutes.

From Hanover NH

I-91 North to Exit 14, then VT-113 West. 28 miles, approximately 40 minutes.

By train

Amtrak to White River Junction. Arrange transport from there — we can suggest options.

Strafford, Vermont

44.03°N 72.36°W

Directions provided above · GPS coordinates 44.0287, -72.3641

Open Days 2025

On open days, the farm is accessible 10am – 4pm, no reservation required. Members may also visit by appointment on any day the farm is operating.

Saturday, 17 May

Spring planting open day

Saturday, 14 June

The seed garden in early bloom

Saturday, 19 July

Peak season — hollyhocks and marigolds

Saturday, 16 August

Harvest begins — dried flower workshop

Saturday, 20 September

Autumn gathering — seed-saving demonstration

Saturday, 18 October

Closing day — the last harvest

Member appointments

Cultivator and Patron members may visit on any working day by appointment. Write to us at hello@marigoldsociety.com at least one week in advance. Patron members are welcome to stay for lunch.

What you'll find

The Seed Garden

Four acres of heirloom varieties at various stages of growth. Labeled with variety name, provenance, and year of introduction. The hollyhocks reach their peak in late July — they are remarkable.

The Cutting Garden

A half-acre garden grown specifically for the pressed-flower kits and dried arrangements. Staffed on open days by our flower manager, Isabelle Renaud, who will discuss technique with anyone who asks.

The Seed Barn

The original dairy barn, converted to seed-drying and printing. The Risograph press where the Almanac is printed is in here, and we run the press on most open days.

The Farm Stand

A small stand with seeds, dried arrangements, and the current issue of the Almanac. Credit cards accepted. We often have produce from the kitchen garden as well.

Workshops

Third Saturday of each month, June – September

Seed Saving for Beginners

A two-hour workshop covering the fundamentals of seed selection, harvest, processing, and storage. Led by Estelle.

$65Enquire →

Select Saturdays, July – October

The Art of Pressing

A half-day workshop on the practice of flower pressing — technique, plant selection, drying, mounting, and archiving. Led by Isabelle Renaud.

$85Enquire →

By arrangement, May – August

Reading the Garden

A private tour of the farm with Oliver or Estelle, focused on garden history and the stories behind the varieties. Groups up to six.

$120 / personEnquire →

The farm smells different in every month it is open. May is earth and cold; June is grass and the first marigolds; July and August are the full abundance; September is dry seed heads and the beginning of wood smoke. October is the best of all.

— Estelle Marchand, Almanac No. 4

The Marigold Society

A Vermont seed library, pressed-flower atelier, and quarterly journal for those who believe the old ways are worth preserving.

Find Us

18 Old Mill Road
Strafford, Vermont 05072
